title: A Belgian wins the 80 million euro EuroMillions jackpot this Friday: here are the numbers you needed to play

world.title: A Belgian wins the 80 million euro EuroMillions jackpot this Friday: here are the numbers you needed to play

environment.compositionIntent: A pale matte paper surround preserves Reinhardt-like active negative space, faint grid seams, dark muted-blue anchors, burgundy compartments, and restrained blue/yellow reveal pressure while leaving the accepted central grid panel and foreground mark field readable.

distanceSafety.reason: Canonical public camera retained as the fixed design bookmark: the accepted parts are broad frontal abstract fields, and preview evidence supports a central pressure panel with surrounding negative space rather than a close portrait crop.

contrast.notes: Neutral paper light keeps pale compartments open while a low cool rim separates charcoal, burgundy, and blue marks without adding a generic glow layer.

paramSemantics.band: visual placement/readability band for full-world params; distinct from declared compositionOwnership.lane

owns: background atmosphere, palette/value field, shallow depth, and active negative space
surrounding WebGPU-safe environment behavior that gives other parts room to read
avoid: main news-pressure band unless explicitly assigned
main knot or focal tangle unless explicitly assigned

compositionAdaptations: Kept at the origin as the accepted background shell and surrounding WebGPU-safe negative-space field.

World palette and low motion preserve the accepted pale paper grid, shallow depth, dark anchors, burgundy/blue compartments, faint reveal slots, and matte tooth without assigning the environment the main news-pressure band.
Kept at origin as the background shell and active negative-space field; softened motion and palette around pale paper, dark anchors, burgundy/blue compartments, and restrained reveal slots.
composition modified world-environment from isolated accepted-part defaults
workshopAcceptance: Inspected advisory concept image directly; used only broad pale paper grid, dark anchors, burgundy/blue accents, and matte density cues.
Static check succeeded for drafts/world-environment.module.mjs with validation pass true and no errors.
Playwright preview attempt 1 succeeded with healthStatus ok and captureStatus ok.
Desktop screenshot showed a full enclosing Reinhardt-like matte paper grid environment with readable dark anchors, burgundy and blue compartments, faint reveal slots, and active negative space.
Render report confirmed rendererMode webgpu, rendererBackend webgpu, browserErrors null, failureReason null.
Quality audit reported nonBlankRatio 1, centerNonBlankRatio 1, dynamicRange 206, paintedCoverageRatio 0.931471.
WebGPU feature facts included utilities.createDirectionSpaceSkyboxShell, sky-background-field, color-field, procedural-marks, soft-particles, and standard WebGPU-safe materials.

owns: middleground selected-news pressure structure: dominant band, grid compression, barricade, or equivalent pressure form
avoid: full foreground artist mark field of loops, scratches, drips, splatters, and near-line behavior

compositionAdaptations: Held in the declared middleground ownership lane as the primary selected-news pressure structure.

Kept near center and scaled to occupy the design frame as a frontal Reinhardt panel with a dominant dark band, compressed grid, burgundy winning cluster, blue reveal cells, and pale relief compartments.
Motion intensity is restrained to the brief's steady-grid-pulse so the EuroMillions event reads as concentrated pressure without literal numbers or decorative movement.
Held in the middleground lane as the primary news signal, centered and scaled for a readable frontal pressure panel with dark band, compressed grid, winning-cluster blocks, and blue reveal cuts.
composition modified non-literal-grid-pressure from isolated accepted-part defaults
workshopAcceptance: Inspected the advisory concept-reference image directly and used it only for broad pale grid, dark interruptions, burgundy/blue accents, and matte rectangular mood.
Static check passed for drafts/non-literal-grid-pressure.module.mjs with no validation errors.

Attempt 2 Playwright desktop preview captured previews/attempt-002/002-non-literal-grid-pressure-draft-desktop.jpg: enlarged frontal Reinhardt-like grid panel is nonblank, readable, central, and visibly organized around dark grid compression, burgundy/blue winning compartments, and restrained reveal slots.

Attempt 2 render report previews/attempt-002/desktop-render-report.json reports healthStatus ok, captureStatus ok, rendererMode webgpu, actualRendererBackend webgpu, and WebGPU-safe MeshBasicMaterial/LineBasicMaterial plus color-field/procedural-marks evidence.

owns: middleground selected-news pressure structure: dominant band, grid compression, barricade, or equivalent pressure form
avoid: full foreground artist mark field of loops, scratches, drips, splatters, and near-line behavior

owns: foreground artist mark and line behavior: loops, scratches, drips, splatters, dot/grid catches, and near readable marks
avoid: another dominant middleground news-pressure band, barricade/grid, or knot structure

compositionAdaptations: Kept in the declared foreground lane for readable artist behavior: pale near compartments, hard-edge catches, contour arcs, scratches, dot/grid catches, burgundy/blue reveal marks, and short helper-backed bars.
Offset slightly right and lower so it overlaps the pressure panel as near surface attention without becoming a second dominant middleground barricade.
Opacity and scale are held below the primary grid signal to preserve Reinhardt's severe frontal order and keep the distributed marks readable on desktop and mobile.
Kept in the foreground lane, slightly right/lower and below the primary signal in opacity so the near scratches, arcs, dots, and hard-edge catches texture the panel without replacing it.
composition modified artist-mark-field from isolated accepted-part defaults
workshopAcceptance: Inspected the provided local concept-reference image directly; used it only for pale matte grid value, dark blue/burgundy accents, fine seams, and sparse arc density.
Static validation succeeded for drafts/artist-mark-field.module.mjs with no errors.
Preview attempt 2 rendered successfully in WebGPU mode with healthStatus ok and captureStatus ok.

Attempt 2 screenshot shows a larger foreground field of pale Reinhardt-like compartments, charcoal/burgundy/blue hard-edge catches, contour arcs, scratch marks, and dot/grid catches without recreating the accepted middleground pressure panel.

Attempt 2 render report recorded WebGPU-safe MeshBasicMaterial, LineBasicMaterial, PointsMaterial, material-factory userdata for color-field/procedural-marks/soft-particles, and webgpu-native-helper evidence for utilities.createAnimatedInstancedMarkField.

compositionEvidence.draftIntent: Preserve the three accepted WebGPU-compatible Ad Reinhardt modules and assemble them as a pale frontal lottery-pressure world: environment as active negative space, non-literal-grid-pressure as the middleground news signal, and artist-mark-field as the foreground mark system.
rendererCompatibilityPreserved: world-environment keeps accepted MeshBasicMaterial, LineBasicMaterial, PointsMaterial, sky-background-field, color-field, procedural-marks, soft-particles, and direction-space-skybox-shell evidence refs.
non-literal-grid-pressure keeps accepted MeshBasicMaterial, LineBasicMaterial, color-field, and procedural-marks evidence refs.
artist-mark-field keeps accepted MeshBasicMaterial, LineBasicMaterial, PointsMaterial, procedural-marks, soft-particles, and animated-instanced-mark-field evidence refs.

takeaways: Use the canonical fixed camera as the design bookmark and judge both desktop and mobile from that stable authored frame.
Keep color ownership in world.outputColorTransform with a modest contrast, saturation, exposure, and vignette adjustment rather than adding material or post-processing claims.
Validate the final assembly from Playwright screenshots and render reports, not from source inspection alone.
